title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) with our easy recipe. A comforting, flavorful, and healthy soup perfect for any meal.

Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup step by step
best Turkish lentil soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Tips for the Best Turkish Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Warm and comforting, this authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) is a staple in Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and comforting flavor. This hearty soup is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a starter or a light meal. Its smooth texture and aromatic spices make it incredibly satisfying and nourishing.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.

instructions.title: Saute Onion and Garlic
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Flour
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Sprinkle the flour over the mixture and cook for another minute, stirring to combine and cook out the raw flour taste.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Liquids
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, stirring to coat. Pour in the chicken or vegetable broth and water. Season with salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup to Smoothness
instructions.text: Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Adjust seasoning if needed.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spiced Butter Topping
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual serving bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the spiced butter topping over each bowl. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side, allowing each person to squeeze lemon juice into their soup to taste.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, you can add half a carrot or a small potato, finely diced, along with the lentils. They will blend seamlessly into the soup.
The quality of your tomato paste makes a difference. Use a good quality, concentrated Turkish tomato paste for authentic flavor.
Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. The acidity from the lemon brightens the soup and is essential for its traditional taste.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey with Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, comforting, and incredibly flavorful soup is a staple in Turkish households, gracing tables from bustling city restaurants to humble village kitchens. Known for its creamy texture, rich aroma, and nourishing qualities, Mercimek Corbasi offers a taste of authentic Turkish hospitality in every spoonful. Whether you're seeking a warm embrace on a chilly day or a light yet satisfying meal, this iconic soup is a perfect choice, embodying the simple elegance and robust flavors of Turkish cuisine.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi?
Mercimek Corbasi (pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-SUH) literally translates to "lentil soup." While there are various lentil soups in Turkey, when most Turks refer to "mercimek corbasi," they are almost always speaking of the red lentil version. This soup is a cornerstone of Turkish gastronomy, often served as a starter to almost any meal, or even as a light lunch on its own. It's renowned for its beautiful reddish-orange hue, derived from the red lentils and a touch of tomato paste, and its smooth, velvety consistency, typically achieved through blending. Beyond its delicious taste, Mercimek Corbasi is celebrated for its nutritional value, packed with protein and fiber, making it both wholesome and deeply satisfying. It represents the warmth and generosity inherent in Turkish food culture, a dish that brings people together.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Creating an authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup requires a few simple yet essential ingredients. The beauty of this soup lies in the quality and freshness of its components, allowing their natural flavors to shine.

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. These quick-cooking lentils break down easily, giving the soup its characteristic creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic base, providing depth and fundamental flavor.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A crucial ingredient that adds a concentrated umami richness and contributes to the soup's signature color.
*   **Butter (or Olive Oil):** Used for sautéing and often for the final flavor-boosting drizzle. Butter adds a wonderful richness.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** A non-negotiable spice in Turkish cooking, especially for soups. It imparts a refreshing, earthy note.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber) or Paprika:** For a gentle warmth and color. Pul Biber offers a mild, fruity heat.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season and enhance all the other flavors.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid base. Broth will add more depth of flavor.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Essential for serving, providing a bright, acidic counterpoint that elevates the entire dish.

## Step-by-Step Mercimek Corbasi Recipe

Yields: 6 servings
Prep time: 10 minutes
Cook time: 30 minutes

**Ingredients:**
*   1 cup (200g) red lentils, rinsed thoroughly
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   2 cloves garlic, minced
*   2 tablespoons butter or olive oil
*   1 tablespoon tomato paste
*   1 teaspoon dried mint
*   1/2 - 1 teaspoon red pepper flakes (pul biber) or paprika, to taste
*   6 cups (1.5 liters) water or vegetable broth
*   Salt and black pepper to taste
*   Lemon wedges for serving

**Instructions:**

1.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the butter (or heat oil) over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
2.  **Add Tomato Paste & Spices:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, allowing it to deepen in color and intensify in flavor. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (or paprika) and stir for about 30 seconds until aromatic. Be careful not to burn the spices.
3.  **Introduce Lentils & Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
4.  **Blend (Optional but Recommended):** For a smooth, creamy Turkish Red Lentil Soup, remove the pot from the heat and carefully blend the soup using an immersion blender until completely smooth. Alternatively, transfer batches of the soup to a regular blender (be very cautious when blending hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
5.  **Season & Adjust Consistency:** Return the soup to low heat if needed. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Simmer for another 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
6.  **Prepare the Mint Oil Drizzle (Optional but Highly Recommended):** While the soup simmers, in a small pan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium-low heat. Add 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint and a pinch of red pepper flakes (if desired). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm mint butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Lentil Soup

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Turkish Red Lentil Soup is easier than you think with a few simple tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils:** Always rinse red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. This removes any dust or debris and can help prevent excessive foam during cooking.
*   **Quality Tomato Paste:** Invest in good quality Turkish tomato paste (salça) if possible. It has a richer, more concentrated flavor than some generic brands and is key to the authentic taste.
*   **Don't Skip the Blending:** While you can technically eat the soup unblended, the signature creamy texture of Mercimek Corbasi comes from blending. An immersion blender makes this step incredibly easy. If using a regular blender, blend in small batches and vent the lid to allow steam to escape.
*   **The Mint Oil Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** This final flourish is non-negotiable for an authentic experience. The sizzled butter with dried mint (and often red pepper flakes) adds an incredible layer of aroma, flavor, and visual appeal that truly elevates the Turkish Red Lentil Soup.
*   **Lemon is Key:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges. A generous squeeze of lemon juice just before eating brightens the soup, cuts through its richness, and balances the flavors beautifully.

## Serving Suggestions for Mercimek Corbasi

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in several ways, reflecting its status as a beloved Turkish staple.

*   **With Lemon:** As mentioned, fresh lemon wedges are paramount. The tartness of the lemon is the perfect counterpoint to the earthy richness of the lentils.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A thick slice of fresh, crusty bread (especially Turkish pide or simit) is ideal for dipping and soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Chili Flakes:** For those who enjoy a bit more heat, a sprinkle of extra pul biber (Turkish red pepper flakes) directly into the bowl adds a pleasant kick.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** A garnish of fresh parsley or mint can add a touch of freshness and color.
*   **As a Starter:** Traditionally, it's often served as the first course of a larger Turkish meal, preparing the palate for what's to come.
*   **Light Lunch:** Paired with a simple salad, Mercimek Corbasi makes a perfectly satisfying and wholesome lunch.

## Variations & Dietary Adaptations

One of the beautiful aspects of Turkish Red Lentil Soup is its inherent flexibility and suitability for various dietary needs.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** This soup is naturally vegetarian. To make it vegan, simply use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and the final drizzle, and ensure you're using vegetable broth or water.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free. As long as you use gluten-free broth (if using) and avoid any gluten-containing garnishes, this soup is entirely suitable for a gluten-free diet.
*   **Adding Vegetables:** While the classic recipe is simple, some variations include finely diced carrots or potatoes cooked along with the lentils. This adds extra nutrients and a slightly different texture.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) to your preference. For a milder soup, omit them entirely or use sweet paprika. For more heat, increase the amount.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth to the soup, though it's not traditional.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's an experience. It's a taste of Turkish comfort, warmth, and culinary tradition, all in one bowl. With its simple ingredients and straightforward preparation, you can easily bring the authentic flavors of Turkey into your home. So go ahead, gather your ingredients, and prepare to be enchanted by the timeless appeal of this exquisite Turkish Red Lentil Soup. Afiyet Olsun (Bon Appétit)!
